title: leucanthemum vulgare var. meridionale
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare var. meridionale, also known as the Mediterranean daisy, is a variant of the common daisy that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare var. meridionale include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
